recommended_use: Primary void catalog for representative cosmic void selection and stacked baseline construction
survey_sample: DESI Year 1 BGS Bright volume-limited sample to z=0.24
main_files_or_products: DESIVAST_BGS_VOLLIM_VoidFinder_NGC.fits; DESIVAST_BGS_VOLLIM_VoidFinder_SGC.fits; DESIVAST_BGS_VOLLIM_V2_VIDE_NGC.fits; DESIVAST_BGS_VOLLIM_V2_VIDE_SGC.fits; DESIVAST_BGS_VOLLIM_V2_REVOLVER_NGC.fits; DESIVAST_BGS_VOLLIM_V2_REVOLVER_SGC.fits
key_columns_or_quantities: EFFECTIVE_RADIUS; EFFECTIVE_RADIUS_UNCERT; EDGE_AREA; TOT_AREA; algorithm-specific void geometry columns
mass_info_available_directly: No direct mass column in void catalog itself
effective_structural_dimension_relevance: Provides void center/size/geometry needed to define center-wall-background zones and compute D_w-like radial structural summaries
notes: Best primary starting point because it offers multiple void-finder algorithms on a common DESI Y1 BGS Bright base

dataset_name: Stellar Mass and Emission Line Catalog (DESI DR1)
recommended_use: Primary galaxy stellar-mass proxy catalog for galaxies around/inside selected voids
data_type: Galaxy stellar mass and emission line catalog
survey_sample: All DESI DR1 galaxies with reliable redshift measurements
key_columns_or_quantities: TARGETID; TARGET_RA; TARGET_DEC; MASS_CG; photometric fluxes; emission line measurements
effective_structural_dimension_relevance: Enables mass-weighted or mass-volume proxy layers after cross-matching with void-environment galaxies
notes: DESI recommends MASS_CG from CIGALE as the stellar-mass field

recommended_use: Secondary environmental mass proxy for wall/filament/group environment around voids
survey_sample: Legacy Surveys DR9-based galaxy/group catalog with DESI redshifts updated to DR1 iron release
key_columns_or_quantities: RA; DEC; Z; IGRP; GRP_RA; GRP_DEC; GRP_Z; GRP_LOGM; GRP_LOGL
volume_info_available: Indirectly via positions/redshifts, not a void-volume field
effective_structural_dimension_relevance: Useful for checking whether void walls/background transitions align with group-rich, higher-mass environments
notes: Good secondary support layer rather than the first dataset to start from

dataset_name: SDSS DR7 VoidFinder Catalog (Pan et al. 2012)
recommended_use: Fallback/reference public void catalog with well-known literature baseline
provider: SDSS DR7 / Pan et al. public void catalog
main_files_or_products: Public VoidFinder void and void-galaxy catalog products described in Pan et al. 2012
key_columns_or_quantities: Void positions; effective radii; void galaxy membership (catalog-dependent)
effective_structural_dimension_relevance: Excellent literature-calibrated fallback for void geometry and stacked radial profile comparisons
notes: Strong literature anchor, but less convenient than DESI DR1 for unified mass-plus-void workflow

dataset_name: SDSS DR7 Watershed/ZOBOV-based Public Void Catalog (Sutter et al. 2012; Nadathur & Hotchkiss 2014 lineage)
recommended_use: Alternative non-spherical public void catalog for robustness checks on void-definition dependence
survey_sample: SDSS DR7 main and LRG samples depending on catalog version
main_files_or_products: Catalog products described in Sutter et al. 2012 and later public watershed-based SDSS DR7 releases
key_columns_or_quantities: Effective radius; redshift; member zones/cells; radial profile products (catalog-dependent)
effective_structural_dimension_relevance: Useful to test whether D_w-style conclusions are robust against non-spherical void definitions
notes: Recommended only after the primary DESI DR1 pipeline is stable
